Complete UX design specification for PYGLARA website and investor document

Full UX spec covering: print-first platform strategy (investor doc as Priority 1),
Path Robotics-inspired dark design direction, 7 user journeys with gap analysis,
11-rule minimal UX pattern set, Astro framework decision (ADR-001/002),
component specs (WhatsApp float, bottom tab bar, service grid, forms),
responsive/accessibility requirements, and validation against PRD.

over hamburger nav, zero client-side JS for core function, navy/copper/green
color palette, capability statement as primary viral distribution asset.

commit.message:
test(tantivy): add unit tests for tokenizer pure functions

Add 18 focused unit tests for pure functions identified in the code
review as lacking dedicated test coverage:

- ascii_fold: Latin-1 Supplement mappings, passthrough, mixed strings (7 tests)
- strip_punctuation: custom chars, no-op on alpha, Unicode, single-char (4 tests)
- english_stop_words: count (33), known entries, no duplicates (3 tests)
- is_stop_word: case sensitivity, empty string, partial match, empty list (4 tests)

AMPHTT-762
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: D+1 deduplication by sale ID instead of comprovante value

The previous dedup logic used banco+cliente+comprovante+data as key,
which incorrectly skipped separate purchases with the same amount
(e.g. 2 identical products bought separately by the same customer).
Now deduplicates by sale ID, ensuring each sale record counts once.

Validated against manual audit: Itaú R$8.243,69 ✓ Infinite R$25.530,10 ✓

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: sounddevice full-duplex + systemd deps + daemon TTS

Audio:
- Replace aplay subprocess with sounddevice (sd_audio.py module)
- Full-duplex: no more ALSA conflict, no pkill arecord needed
- Play latency: 270ms → 128ms
- E2E "salut": 3s → 2.6s

TTS daemon:
- Paroli daemon mode with non-blocking IO (select+fcntl)
- TTS synthesis: 1500ms → 250ms (model stays in memory)

Systemd:
- diva-server depends on paroli-tts (not piper-tts)
- All services properly ordered: paroli→audio→memory→rkllama→server
- intent-router: SO_REUSEADDR + ExecStartPre pkill

commit.message:
fix: packaging save only persisting first item and weight input precision

tempId from DB was stored as a number but compared as a string in the
save logic, causing all existing items to be deleted and re-inserted on
every save. Also increased net weight input step from 0.01 to 0.0001
to support 4 decimal places (e.g. label weights like 0.0018g).

commit.message:
refactor: migrate CRA to Vite, modernize architecture, clean up codebase

- Migrate from ejected CRA (webpack/babel) to Vite with @vitejs/plugin-react
- Remove ~45 unused CRA/webpack/babel dependencies
- Delete ejected config/ and scripts/ directories, CRA boilerplate files
- Rename all .js to .jsx (Vite convention for JSX files)
- Delete 10+ dead/unreachable files (Login, Sparkline, PoolOverview, BridgeIcon,
  LiquidityManagement, ManageLP, Analytics, layout/Layout)

Architecture:
- Create WalletContext replacing React.cloneElement prop drilling
- Create LoadingContext replacing DOM-based showLoadingScreen
- Create useTransaction hook unifying StatusModal boilerplate
- Create useErthPrice, useIsMobile shared hooks
- Create formatUtils (formatPrice, formatCompact) replacing 4x duplication
- Add clearLoginPermit helper, merge duplicate queryRegistry functions
- Delete uiUtils.js (replaced by LoadingContext)
- Delete swapTokensUtils.js (moved to mathUtils)

CSS:
- Migrate 9 files to CSS Modules with camelCaseOnly convention
- Centralize Google Fonts + global reset in index.css
- Delete ~600 lines of dead CSS from fund pages and App.css
- Custom ERTH/ANML orbit loading animation replacing loading.gif

Fixes:
- Fix feeDenom bug in contractUtils snip() ("scrt" -> "uscrt")
- Fix component/file name mismatches (ReusableModal->StatusModal, StakingManagement->StakeErth)
- Fix inconsistent transaction feedback (StakeErth mixed StatusModal and showLoadingScreen)
- Remove 5 dead exports from contractUtils
- Add Vite dev proxy for CORS in development
- Update Dockerfile for Vite build

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Phase 1 platform shell (templates/platform.html, /platform + /fragment routes)
- 6-tier web scraper fallback (Firecrawl -> Jina -> Tavily -> LLM -> Cache -> stdlib)
- Nova Enter key fix v4 (3s threshold, toast, try/catch nuclear fallback)
- Nova chatbot auto-inject on all template pages
- Enrichment code deduplicated (-202 lines) + parallelized (ThreadPoolExecutor)
- Graceful LLM fallback when all 24 providers fail
- History cap increased 6->20 turns, client-side 4K char cap
- XSS fix: javascript/data/vbscript URL protocol blocking
- Canvas 3D orb pauses when panel closed (CPU/battery save)
- Joveo category spend data (433 subcategories, $16M, Oct25-Feb26)
- Deleted dead nova_voice.py (stdlib violation, superseded by ElevenLabs)
- 6 docs: audit, blueprints, stress test report, Slack guide, ComplianceGuard brief

fix(signals): fix LONG-only bias and side propagation bugs

- states.py: market_direction() now uses 5-candle averages instead of
  single candle comparison — eliminates noise sensitivity that caused
  DOWN to be detected ~3x less often than UP

- signal_generator.py: add "side" key to signal_data dict and to both
  SignalSnapshot constructors (temp + final) — was missing entirely,
  causing Risk Core to always evaluate trades as SHORT

- core/signal_snapshot.py: add side: Optional[str] = None field to
  SignalSnapshot dataclass

- execution/gatekeeper.py: _check_risk_core() fallback now infers side
  from stop position (LONG: stop below entry, SHORT: stop above) when
  signal_data["side"] is absent

- journal.py: log_signal_snapshot() uses snapshot.side directly instead
  of inferring direction from tp > entry (fragile inference kept as
  fallback)

commit.message:
perf: daemon mode TTS — 1500ms → 250ms (6x faster)

Paroli TTS switched from fork mode (new process per request, model reload
each time) to daemon mode (persistent process, model stays in memory).

- synthesize() rewritten with non-blocking IO (select + fcntl)
- Fixes the "blocking stdout issue" that kept daemon mode disabled
- First byte latency: ~50ms (was ~1500ms)
- Total synthesis: ~250ms (was ~1500ms)
- Model loaded once at startup, reused for all requests

Also: intent-router SO_REUSEADDR + conversational regex rules cleanup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
update: replace Jigsaw 3 papers with applied causal forest studies

Swap out 2 weak papers (Basu 2007 predates CF, Athey & Imbens 2016 purely
methodological) for 4 applied papers using causal forests on real health data:
Inoue (Medicaid/CV risk), Boutilier (TB targeting), Venkatasubramaniam
(diabetes CF vs regression), McCoy (septic shock triangulation). Reshuffle
expert groups so no student repeats Jigsaw 2 groupmates. Remove draft status.

commit.message:
Fix raid panel bugs: spam-create, JSON error, UX reflow

Bug fixes:
- Disable Create/Link buttons during raid playlist creation to
  prevent spam-duplicate playlists on rapid clicks
- Add content-type check to raid-status fetch to catch HTML
  responses from session-expired redirects (prevents
  "Unexpected token '<'" JSON parse errors)

UX improvements per user feedback:
- Move drip config to its own section between sources and schedule
  (was buried inside the raid link card)
- Drip section shows: enabled toggle + "Add N tracks per run"
- Clearer source card labels: "Raid N tracks per run" instead of
  "pull N tracks"
- Panel flow now matches the multi-step process:
  1. Raid Playlist (create/link)
  2. External Sources (per-source N)
  3. Drip to Playlist (how many → target)
  4. Schedule (when)
  5. Raid Now + Drip Now

commit.message:
feat: offline WiFi attack mode (Pwnagotchi-inspired) + kernel prep

Offline mode pipeline: scan → select target (ROE) → capture handshake → crack → connect.
Uses external USB WiFi adapter (wlan1) for monitor mode — internal QCA6390 cannot
do monitor mode on kernel 4.19.157-perf+. wlan0 stays connected (Tailscale/SSH up).

New files:
- agent/offline_manager.py — pipeline state machine, simulation mode, subprocess mgmt
- tests/test_offline_api.py — 60 API tests (full sim pipeline)
- tests/test_offline_ui.py — 38 Playwright UI tests (panels, transitions)
- offline-plan.md — living plan with decisions, progress log, lessons learned
- kernels/README.md — kernel flash instructions + recovery steps
- scripts/test-monitor.sh, test-pmkid.sh — standalone WiFi test scripts
- scripts/cron-monitor-prompt.md — saved cron monitor prompt

Modified:
- agent/db.py — wifi_networks + wifi_handshakes tables
- agent/net_detect.py — is_wifi_connected() function
- webui/server.py — 14 /api/offline/* endpoints, init_state on import
- webui/templates/index.html — offline UI (amber theme, Pwnagotchi face, network
  cards, handshake monitor, crack progress, WiFi feed, adapter status)
- scripts/llama-watchdog.sh — pause file support (/data/local/tmp/nc-llama-pause)
- .gitignore — kernels/*.img, *.zip
- docs: CLAUDE.md, README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, FEATURES.md updated

Lessons learned: QCA6390 monitor mode impossible (airmon-ng rc=1, iw EOPNOTSUPP,
ip link silent fail, hcxdumptool "failed to arm"). External adapter required.
NetHunter kernel (flypatriot crDroid A12) downloaded to /sdcard/nightcrawler-kernels/.

and config section for adjustable thresholds (60/50°C).

commit.message:
fix: handle Windows Terminal trailing whitespace stripping in paste events (#58)

Windows Terminal strips trailing whitespace from each line and trailing
newlines when forwarding clipboard content via bracketed paste. This
caused _on_paste to treat the paste as external, overwriting the local
clipboard (losing trailing spaces) and resetting the line-copy flag
(breaking VS Code line-paste behavior).

Use a trailing-whitespace-tolerant comparison (rstrip per line after
rstrip on the whole string) so that whitespace-only differences
preserve the local clipboard and line-copy state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: skip Qwen intent, fast routing, mem0 timeout, anti-TV, conversational→Claude

Intent router:
- Skip Qwen NPU pass 2 (780ms→0.1ms) — regex miss goes straight to Claude
- conversational regex rules added then removed — all goes to Claude
- SO_REUSEADDR for intent-router restart resilience
- Greeting/conversational patterns for regex fast-path

Latency:
- Mem0 recall timeout: 2000ms → 500ms
- SYNTHESIZE_TIMEOUT_MS: 3s → 8s (Paroli NPU needs margin)
- Processing feedback disabled (audio lock conflict)

Audio:
- ALSA half-duplex: kill arecord before aplay + retry loop
- Micro muted during entire streaming TTS session
- Zombie arecord cleanup via os.waitpid()
- TTS cache with auto pre-warm at startup (16 phrases)

Anti-TV:
- Wake word threshold: 0.70 → 0.85 (guest)
- Adult threshold: 0.90 → 0.92
- Energy minimum filter: reject < -20dB (distant sources)

Routing:
- No hardcoded conversational responses — all goes to Claude
- local_simple intent recognized by Node.js
- silencieux mode blocked without audio metadata

repository.description: GAPIv2 - Multi-channel communication platform with WhatsApp, Facebook, Instagram, and Web Widget support

commit.message:
Add TokBurn-inspired waste features: per-file reads, project waste, waste score

- Parser extracts per-file read counts from Read/ReadFile/View tool_use blocks
- New session_file_reads table tracks which files were read how many times
- Waste tab: per-project waste breakdown with bar chart, file re-read hotspots (3x+)
- Sessions table: waste score column (0-100) with native tooltip showing contributors
- Score combines 4 signals: cost outlier (0-40), floundering (0-25), compaction (0-20), file rereads (0-15)
- Fixes from 3-agent Opus review: Fragment keys, CSV export, tooltip clipping, sort disabled

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
